text: A pet shop has a total of 150 fish split between two tanks. Tank A contains 22 more fish than Tank B. How many fish are in Tank A?
generatorSolution: Let the number of fish in Tank B = x. Then Tank A = x + 22. Together they total 150, so: x + (x + 22) = 150. This gives 2x + 22 = 150. Subtract 22 from both sides: 2x = 128. Divide both sides by 2: x = 64. So Tank B has 64 fish and Tank A has 64 + 22 = 86 fish.
